div.h1: Performance Benchmark: WAL Group Commit Implementation
div.p: Test Environment: 100 Concurrent Write Requests • 10 Active Worker Threads • NVMe SSD (Avg Latency 2ms) Metric: Total Latency per 100 Transactions (Amortized vs. Individual)

ul.li: Baseline Path: Thread acquires mutex → appends to WAL → calls fsync() → releases mutex.
Optimized Path: Thread appends to buffer → enters 'Wait Queue' → Leader Thread performs single fsync() for all buffered transactions → signals completion to followers.

div.p: Implementation Insight: The system utilizes a Fixed-Window Batching algorithm. The leader thread waits for either 1ms or 16 pending transactions before triggering the hardware flush to maximize SATA/NVMe bandwidth utilization.
